2606.09266.title: Physics-Guided Sequence-Based Generative Framework for Acoustic Metamaterial Inverse Design
2606.09266.paper_summary: Acoustic metamaterial (AMM) inverse design is particularly challenging for broadband target responses due to acoustic dispersion: a structure that matches the desired response at one frequency may deviate at others, and modifying geometry to improve one sub-band often perturbs neighboring sub-bands. Yet existing broadband inverse-design approaches are either constrained by predefined templates, or rely on image representations that fail to preserve the geometric precision and structural connectivity required by acoustic structures. We present MetaSeq, a physics-guided, sequence-based generative framework for acoustic metamaterial inverse design. At its core, MetaSeq introduces a language that represents each AMM as a structured sequence, rather than as a pixel grid or fixed template. This representation preserves precise geometry, explicitly encodes connectivity, and casts inverse design as a sequence-to-sequence task from target response to structure sequence. MetaSeq further constructs a balanced, high-fidelity dataset with efficient calibration and complexity-based sampling. To address the one-to-many nature of inverse design, MetaSeq combines supervised pretraining with reinforcement learning fine-tuning guided by a physics-based solver and validity checker. Extensive evaluations against COMSOL and five baselines show that MetaSeq reduces response error by 45% over the best baseline.
2606.09266.paper_summary_zh: 聲學超材料（AMM）逆向設計對於寬頻目標響應特別具有挑戰性，這是由於聲學色散：在某一頻率上匹配所需響應的結構在其他頻率上可能會偏離，並且為了改善一個子頻帶而修改幾何形狀往往會擾動相鄰的子頻帶。然而，現有的寬頻逆向設計方法要麼受到預定模板的限制，要麼依賴於無法保留聲學結構所需的幾何精度和結構連通性的圖像表示。我們提出了MetaSeq，一種基於物理引導的序列生成框架，用於聲學超材料的逆向設計。在其核心，MetaSeq引入了一種語言，將每個AMM表示為結構化序列，而不是像素網格或固定模板。這種表示保留了精確的幾何形狀，明確編碼了連通性，並將逆向設計視為從目標響應到結構序列的序列到序列任務。MetaSeq進一步構建了一個平衡的高保真數據集，具有高效的校準和基於複雜性的抽樣。為了應對逆向設計的一對多特性，MetaSeq結合了有監督的預訓練和由基於物理的求解器和有效性檢查器引導的強化學習微調。與COMSOL和五個基準進行的廣泛評估顯示，MetaSeq將響應誤差降低了45%，超過了最佳基準。
2606.09266.author: Yijie Li et.al.
2606.09266.authors: Yijie Li, Jiahao Xu, Ching-Chih Tsao, Lili Qiu, Jingxian Wang
